location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of hub (frequency not specified)

level_signals: 8 - 10 years of experience in strategy and operations roles
Serves as strategic thought partner to senior leaders
Leads large, cross-functional initiatives from concept through execution

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: you should have 8 - 10 years of experience in strategy and operations roles (e.g., consulting, finance, GM, Operations)
Proven experience developing and executing strategies that drive scalable, high-quality operations in ambiguous, fast-paced environments
Strong analytical judgment, with the ability to synthesize quantitative and qualitative insights into clear recommendations and decisions
Demonstrated success leading large, cross-functional initiatives from concept through execution
Ability to identify operational inefficiencies and design scalable processes, systems, and operating models to support long-term growth
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across Product, Engineering, Operations, and senior leadership
Proficiency in data analysis and reporting tools
Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance of our New York, San Francisco, Phoenix, or Los Angeles hubs.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: experience with SQL and analytics platforms (e.g., Looker, Sigma, Hex) is a plus

scope.surface: Post-purchase operations and commerce marketplace ecosystem (buyer experience, seller experience, trust & risk, fraud, logistics, payments)
primary_metrics_inferred: Cross-functional initiative outcomes and measurable results
core_responsibilities: Set and drive the strategy for post-purchase operations, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and Operations to deliver scalable, high-quality experiences for Whatnot's users
Translate insights from operational data and user interactions into priorities, roadmaps, and initiatives that improve quality, speed, and scalability
Lead complex, cross-functional initiatives from problem definition through execution, ensuring alignment, accountability, and measurable outcomes
Design and implement scalable processes, systems, and operating models that support Whatnot's growth and evolving user needs
Serve as a strategic thought partner to senior leaders, providing data-driven recommendations and tradeoffs to inform decision-making
Continuously assess operational performance, identifying opportunities to improve efficiency, resilience, and user experience

green_flags: Forbes #1 Best Startup Employer in America — external validation of employer brand
Comprehensive benefits package including 401k match, parental leave, wellness, childcare, and family planning
Clear team scope and cross-functional partners named
Remote co-located model with hub options across multiple US cities
red_flags: Requires physical presence within commuting distance of NYC, SF, Phoenix, or LA hubs — eliminates fully remote candidates despite 'remote co-located' framing
Title spans Manager to Senior Manager with no clarity on leveling criteria or comp differentiation between the two
Role described as 'remote co-located' but commuting distance is required — framing may be misleading

scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2C marketplace/livestream shopping)

ambiguities: Role title is 'Manager/Senior Manager' — JD does not clarify criteria for one level vs the other, nor comp differences between them.

JD describes Whatnot as 'a remote co-located team' but simultaneously requires candidates to be within commuting distance of a hub. This may mean in-office frequency is low but location is constrained. Treated as hybrid.
manages_people is marked false because the JD does not mention direct reports, but a Manager/Senior Manager title could imply future or current people management.
